pages.full_text:
& saw [Gol] Stave Church & [barns] & someone spinning. The boys went to the Ship Museum. Then we all went to the Fram Museum & the Kon Tiki museum. They are marvellously laid out.
	We had hoped that the boys could swim at the pool in the park but it was shut. We looked at [Vigeland's] wonderful statues, an obelisk of bodies, [?not seen]. He showed us a restaurant where we had a delicious meal of catfish. John & Charles are fighting Svein Stoesen.
	Thank you for wanting to stop at any house any friends for the jolly good time we had together. I enjoyed every minute of it.  Affectionately, [Svein]

William - Kon Tiki: Heyerdahl burnt the Tigris in protest at the Red Sea War

Peter - Kon Tiki
John - [Gjoa] about fishing & getting fish
Charles - Gjoa - Amundsen's ship, outside & smaller than Fram, captain's cabin.
					[sketcher] [?$11] [?waterfall] [?leads] [?beds] [?free]
Simon - Viking, shapes of boats
Eve - Folkmuseum, peasant's cottage,
	 Viking shape of boats, Fram - Nansen's life, adventurer, ambassador, saved refugees
pages.notes: Page is a continuation; top is clipped, no handwritten page number visible. The passage 'Thank you for wanting to stop...' appears to be a quoted farewell from a host named Svein (surname Stoesen mentioned earlier). The name at the end of the quote reads as '[Svein]' but could be another reading. '[Gol]' at very top likely refers to Gol Stave Church (a Norwegian stave church). 'Vigeland's' likely refers to Vigeland's Sculpture Park in Oslo. Small marginal notations on right side are largely illegible — appear to be a tiny sketch/table with numbers ($11, dimensions?). 'Heyerdahl' refers to Thor Heyerdahl. The name '[Gjoa]' (Gjøa) is Amundsen's ship. Year not determinable from this page alone; Oslo trip context with Fram/Kon Tiki/Viking Ship Museum suggests Norway visit, volume range 1989-2003.

pages.full_text: The Gokstad Ship c 900 found near Oslo Fjord The preserved wood was steamed & boiled in alum & impregnated with linseed oil. Oak ship, ash tiller 32 oarsmen, helmsman, look out. Burial ship for tall, 60 year old, chieftain.
photo_captions: The Gokstad Ship c 900 found near Oslo Fjord The preserved wood was steamed & boiled in alum & impregnated with linseed oil. Oak ship, ash tiller 32 oarsmen, helmsman, look out. Burial ship for tall, 60 year old, chieftain.
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a photograph of the Gokstad Viking ship displayed in a museum (label on base reads 'GOKSTADSKIBET'). The handwritten text below serves as an informational caption. Page number '75' appears to be visible at top right though partially cut. Overflow text from the left page is visible in the left gutter margin and has not been re-transcribed here.
